description: State machine definition for the /debug-test workflow

states.name: Clarification gates
states.instruction: Use AskUserQuestion (load via ToolSearch first) to batch the debug-test clarification gates: (1) reproduction confirmed — yes/no, block until yes; (2) fix scope — this bug only / fix + prevent class / fix + refactor area; (3) prod incident — yes/no, affects urgency and rollback tolerance; (4) rollback acceptable — yes/no; (5) tests allowed to change — none / only broken test / new tests added / refactor existing; (6) root cause ownership — our code / upstream dep / framework / infra; (7) post-mortem required — yes/no. Batch into two calls of ≤3 questions each.
states.exit_condition: All seven clarification questions answered and locked in.

states.name: Enable terse mode
states.instruction: Run /caveman full to enable terse output mode for the session.
states.exit_condition: Caveman terse mode confirmed active.

states.name: Reproduce bug
states.instruction: Ask the user to run the reproduction steps and paste the exact error output. Do not proceed until a concrete error is provided. Block with [BLOCKED: awaiting reproduction] if the user cannot reproduce.
states.exit_condition: User has provided an exact error message confirming reproduction in their environment.

states.name: Capture error verbatim
states.instruction: Ask the user to paste the full stack trace or error log verbatim. Record it exactly — do not paraphrase. This becomes the canonical error signature used to validate the failing test in C13.
states.exit_condition: Full stack trace or error log captured verbatim.

states.name: Detect recent changes
states.instruction: Run gitnexus_detect_changes to identify symbols and files changed recently that may be related to the bug. Summarize the change set — files modified, symbols affected — as context for the investigation phase.
states.exit_condition: Change detection complete; candidate symbols and files identified.

states.name: Impact analysis on suspect symbol
states.instruction: Run gitnexus_impact on the primary suspect symbol identified in C4 with direction upstream. Report blast radius: direct callers, affected processes, risk level. Warn user if risk is HIGH or CRITICAL before proceeding.
states.exit_condition: Blast radius reported; HIGH/CRITICAL risk surfaced to user if present.

states.name: Context on suspect symbol
states.instruction: Run gitnexus_context on the primary suspect symbol to get a 360-degree view: all callers, callees, and execution flows it participates in. Use this to narrow the hypothesis.
states.exit_condition: Symbol context loaded; callers, callees, and execution flows documented.

states.name: Parallel exploration
states.instruction: Launch three parallel exploration agents: C7a (calls/callers), C7b (tests), C7c (recent commits). All three must complete before joining at C8.
states.exit_condition: All three parallel exploration agents complete.

states.name: Explore calls and callers
states.instruction: Trace the call graph around the suspect symbol: who calls it, what it calls, and which execution flows it sits on. Look for unexpected callers or call paths that could introduce the bug.
states.exit_condition: Call graph traced; unexpected callers or paths flagged.

states.name: Explore existing tests
states.instruction: Find all existing tests covering the suspect symbol and related code paths. Identify gaps — which paths have no test coverage — and note tests that should have caught this bug but did not.
states.exit_condition: Existing test coverage mapped; gaps and missed catches documented.

states.name: Explore recent commits
states.instruction: Review git log for the suspect files over the past 30 commits. Look for the change that introduced the regression. Cross-reference with gitnexus_detect_changes output from C4.
states.exit_condition: Commit history reviewed; candidate regression commit identified if present.

states.name: Search memory for similar bugs
states.instruction: Call mem_search with the error signature and suspect symbol name to find similar bugs, past fixes, and relevant learnings from Engram memory.
states.exit_condition: Memory search complete; similar past bugs and fixes surfaced if present.

states.name: Search vault for personal notes
states.instruction: Run /vault-find to search the Brain Obsidian vault for any personal notes, session notes, or code-project notes related to the buggy module or error pattern.
states.exit_condition: Vault search complete; relevant personal notes surfaced if present.

states.name: Architect review if structural smell
states.instruction: If the investigation reveals a structural smell — anti-pattern, layering violation, or design issue contributing to the bug — invoke the architect agent to assess and recommend the minimal structural fix compatible with the agreed fix scope from C0.
states.exit_condition: Architect assessment complete; structural recommendation recorded or skipped if no smell.

states.name: Hypothesis gate
states.instruction: State the root-cause hypothesis in exactly one sentence before proceeding. Example: 'The race condition in processQueue() is caused by missing mutex lock on the shared counter introduced in commit a3f9b2.' The user must confirm or redirect. Do not proceed to Phase 3 without hypothesis confirmation.
states.exit_condition: Root-cause hypothesis stated in one sentence and confirmed by user.

states.name: Write failing test via TDD
states.instruction: Run /tdd to write a failing test that exactly reproduces the bug described by the confirmed hypothesis. The test must fail for the right reason — matching the error signature captured in C3.
states.exit_condition: Failing test written and committed to the test suite.

states.name: Stack additional tests
states.instruction: Write additional unit tests for boundary conditions, null inputs, and edge cases around the bug site. Use the test-coverage skill to guide which paths need coverage.
states.exit_condition: Edge case tests added; coverage extended around the bug site.

states.name: Property-based test for bug class
states.instruction: Write a property-based test that encodes an invariant violated by the bug class. Use the appropriate framework for the language: hypothesis (Python), fast-check (TS/JS), gopter (Go), proptest (Rust). The property test should catch any regression of the same class, not just the specific instance.
states.exit_condition: Property-based test written targeting the invariant violated by this bug class.

states.name: Confirm test RED for right reason
states.instruction: Run the failing test and confirm it fails with the exact error message captured in C3. If the test fails for a different reason, it does not lock the bug — return to C11. User must confirm the failure output matches before proceeding to the fix phase.
states.exit_condition: Test is RED and failure output matches the canonical error signature from C3.

states.name: Apply minimal diff
states.instruction: Apply the smallest possible change that makes the failing test pass. Follow immutable patterns per coding-style.md. Do not refactor, rename, or expand scope beyond what the test requires. One fix, minimal lines.
states.exit_condition: Minimal diff applied; no unrelated changes included.

states.name: Run test — must be GREEN
states.instruction: Run the full test suite. The previously failing test must now pass (GREEN). If any test is still RED, revert the fix and return to C14. User must confirm GREEN status before proceeding. If not green after revert, increment red_loop_count.
states.exit_condition: All tests GREEN including the previously failing test; user confirmed.

states.name: Save fix to memory
states.instruction: Call mem_save to record the confirmed fix: what the bug was, root cause, fix applied, files changed, and which tests now cover it. Use topic key format: project-name/bugfix/symbol-name.
states.exit_condition: Fix recorded in Engram memory with structured metadata.

states.name: Lint and verify
states.instruction: Run lint and /verify to confirm the fix passes all static checks, type checks, and build steps. Fix any lint errors introduced by the diff before proceeding.
states.exit_condition: Lint, types, and build all pass clean.

states.name: Check test coverage
states.instruction: Run /test-coverage to verify coverage is at or above 80% on changed files. If coverage dropped below 80%, add tests before proceeding.
states.exit_condition: Coverage at or above 80% on all changed files.

states.name: Mutation testing
states.instruction: Run mutation testing on changed files only to verify test strength. Target: 60%+ mutation score. Use the correct tool for the language: Stryker (JS/TS), mutmut (Python), go-mutesting (Go), cargo-mutants (Rust). If score is below 60%, strengthen the weakest tests before proceeding.
states.exit_condition: Mutation score at or above 60% on changed files.

states.name: Codex rescue authorization gate
states.instruction: The fix loop has exceeded two RED cycles (red_loop_count > 2). Ask the user via AskUserQuestion whether to invoke Codex rescue for a second opinion, try a different approach here, or escalate to a human. Do not invoke Codex without explicit authorization.
states.exit_condition: User has authorized or declined Codex rescue explicitly.

states.name: Codex rescue
states.instruction: Run /codex:rescue with the bug description, canonical error from C3, confirmed hypothesis from C10.5, and the failing test from C11 as context. Let Codex attempt an independent fix approach.
states.exit_condition: Codex rescue job submitted; job ID recorded.

states.name: Check Codex status
states.instruction: Run /codex:status to poll the Codex job. If still running, wait and poll again. If complete, proceed to C20. If timeout exceeded (5 min), proceed to C21.
states.exit_condition: Codex job status is complete or timeout reached.

states.name: Retrieve Codex result
states.instruction: Run /codex:result to retrieve the Codex fix. Read the proposed diff carefully. Do not auto-apply — surface it for review in C22.
states.exit_condition: Codex result retrieved and diff recorded for review.

states.name: Cancel stuck Codex job
states.instruction: If the Codex job is stuck (no result after timeout, or status is stalled), run /codex:cancel to terminate it cleanly. Skip this state if the job completed normally.
states.exit_condition: Codex job cancelled cleanly or confirmed already complete.

states.name: Codex adversarial review
states.instruction: Run /codex:adversarial-review on the Codex-proposed diff. Present the review output to the user. Never auto-apply the Codex fix — user must explicitly approve before any changes are made. This is a mandatory gate.
states.exit_condition: Adversarial review complete; user has approved or rejected the Codex fix.

states.name: AgentDiet context brief
states.instruction: Compress the trajectory before the review chain. Classify all prior agent outputs: KEEP (findings, decisions, test results, error messages), SUMMARIZE (verbose command output → 1-2 line conclusion), DROP (expired state, superseded plans, duplicate information). Produce a compressed context brief for the review agents.
states.exit_condition: Context brief produced; trajectory compressed to KEEP+SUMMARIZE content only.

states.name: Security review
states.instruction: Invoke the security-reviewer agent on the fix diff. Focus on architectural security that semgrep cannot detect: auth bypass, trust boundary violations, privilege escalation. End output with [CONFIDENCE: HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW]. Apply escalation triggers per performance.md if LOW confidence or CRITICAL finding.
states.exit_condition: Security review complete with confidence tag; CRITICAL issues addressed before proceeding.

states.name: Code review
states.instruction: Run the code-reviewer agent on the full fix diff including tests. Address all CRITICAL and HIGH findings before proceeding. Fix MEDIUM issues where possible. End output with [CONFIDENCE: HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW].
states.exit_condition: Code review complete; CRITICAL and HIGH issues resolved.

states.name: Language-specific reviewer
states.instruction: If a language-specific reviewer exists for this codebase (go-review, python-review, etc.), run it on the fix diff. End output with [CONFIDENCE: HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW]. Apply escalation triggers if confidence is LOW or a CRITICAL finding is raised.
states.exit_condition: Language reviewer complete with confidence tag, or skipped if no reviewer exists for this language.

states.name: Compile final review
states.instruction: Aggregate all review findings from C23, C24, C25. Produce a single summary: issues resolved, issues deferred with rationale, overall confidence. This is the pre-commit sign-off document.
states.exit_condition: Final review summary produced; all unresolved CRITICAL/HIGH issues documented.

states.name: E2E tests
states.instruction: Run /e2e to execute end-to-end tests covering the affected user-facing flows. If any E2E test fails, fix before proceeding. Skip if the fix has no user-facing impact.
states.exit_condition: E2E tests pass or skipped with documented rationale.

states.name: Build fix
states.instruction: If the build is RED after applying the fix, run /build-fix to diagnose and resolve the build failure. Do not proceed to commit until the build is GREEN.
states.exit_condition: Build is GREEN.

states.name: Refactor clean
states.instruction: If the fix touched three or more files, run /refactor-clean to remove dead code, simplify adjacent logic, and ensure no file exceeds 150 LOC. Keep changes in a separate commit from the fix.
states.exit_condition: Dead code cleaned; no file over 150 LOC in touched set.

states.name: Commit with caveman
states.instruction: Run /caveman-commit to stage and commit the fix. Follow conventional commits format: 'fix: <description>'. User must confirm the commit message before it is applied. Do not amend — create a new commit.
states.exit_condition: Fix committed to the branch with a conventional commit message.

states.name: Create pull request
states.instruction: Run gh pr create with a title (under 70 chars), summary of the bug and fix, test plan checklist, and root cause explanation. Use a HEREDOC for the body. Push with -u flag if the branch has no remote tracking branch.
states.exit_condition: Pull request created; PR URL returned to user.

states.name: Checkpoint
states.instruction: Run /checkpoint to save the current session state: branch, PR URL, open items, and next actions. This enables safe session resumption if work is interrupted.
states.exit_condition: Session checkpoint saved.

states.name: Save bugfix to memory
states.instruction: Call mem_save to record the complete bugfix knowledge: bug description, root cause, files changed, fix applied, tests added, PR URL, and any patterns to watch for. Topic key: project-name/bugfix/symbol-name.
states.exit_condition: Bugfix knowledge saved to Engram with full structured metadata.

states.name: Update vault session note
states.instruction: Run /vault-session to update or create the session note in the Brain vault sessions/ folder. Record: bug fixed, approach taken, key learnings, links to PR and relevant code. Append to existing note if session already open.
states.exit_condition: Session note updated in vault with bug fix summary.

states.name: Learn evaluation
states.instruction: Run /learn-eval to evaluate what was learned during this debug session: what worked, what was surprising, what heuristics should be updated. This feeds the continuous learning loop.
states.exit_condition: Learning evaluation complete; insights recorded.

states.name: Session memory summary
states.instruction: Call mem_session_summary to save the end-of-session summary to Engram. This is mandatory before declaring the workflow complete. Include: bug fixed, approach, key decisions, PR URL, and open items if any.
states.exit_condition: Session summary saved to Engram. Workflow complete.
